description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, visually reminiscent of the Thompson 'Tommy Gun' with a robust black metal receiver and barrel, complemented by polished wooden furniture. The stock, foregrip, and pistol grip are all crafted from a warm-toned wood, providing both ergonomic support and a vintage aesthetic. The barrel is long and slender, ending in a simple cylindrical muzzle with no visible attachments. A large, round drum magazine is mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of early 20th-century automatic weapons. The iron sights are integrated into the top of the receiver, and the overall design is utilitarian yet iconic, often associated with historical urban and law enforcement contexts. The combination of wood and metal materials gives the rifle a sturdy, reliable appearance.
description: A vintage-style submachine gun with a black metal body, wooden stock, and a prominent drum magazine. The design features classic iron sights and ergonomic wooden grips.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the wooden stock and grips.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rifle, the wooden stock, foregrip, and the drum magazine beneath the receiver.
description_view_2: Front view emphasizing the round drum magazine, the barrel's end, and the symmetry of the wooden grips.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, foregrip, and the overall profile of the rifle.
